steps.title: Identify the temporal structure
steps.content: ”When we arrived” is a past-tense dependent clause introducing the main clause beginning with “we.”

steps.title: Apply verb tense consistency
steps.content:
Verbs in dependent and main clauses must match in tense. “Noticed” (past, choice D) aligns with “arrived” to show both actions occurred simultaneously.

Choices A, B, and C shift to present, present continuous, or future tense, breaking the required consistency.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
